title: How You Actually Spend Your Life

beats.narration: You are going to spend twenty-six years of your life asleep. And that is just the beginning of where it all goes.
beats.understand: a shocking number to open — 26 years asleep

flat.label: in one lifetime, you will spend

beats.narration: Here is where the rest of it actually goes. Thirteen years at work. Eleven years staring at a screen. Years more just eating, cleaning, and commuting.
beats.understand: the hero breakdown — a comparison of life's big time-sinks

beats.narration: Look at that phone number again. Eleven years. More than a decade of your one life, gone into a screen in your hand.
beats.understand: the gut-punch — 11 years on a phone

flat.label: more than a decade of your life

beats.narration: So after everything you have to do, everything that is already spoken for, you are left with roughly nine years of truly free time. That is all you get.
beats.understand: the scarcity reveal — only ~9 years of free time

flat.label: after the rest is subtracted, you have

beats.narration: Nine years. That is the part of your life that is actually yours to choose. Spend it on something that matters.

flat.statement: Nine years are yours. Spend them well.
